concerns.text: Standard logistic regression assumes independent observations, but code units from the same project are correlated (shared team, coding standards, age). This violates independence assumptions and inflates Type I error rates. Consider mixed-effects models with project as random effect, or cluster-robust standard errors.

Bug-fix labels from commit messages have known precision issues (assumed ≥85% in spec). No sensitivity analysis planned for label noise impact on effect estimates. Should include robustness checks varying label quality assumptions.

Power analysis deferred to implementation phase without commitment to conduct before final conclusions. Should explicitly schedule power analysis to ensure ≥10,000 code units is adequate for expected effect sizes.
